The apostles preached about Jesus’ resurrection from the dead. It includes all the happiness of the future state; this they preached through Jesus Christ, to be had through him only. Miserable is their case, for whom the glory of Christ's kingdom is a grief; for since the glory of that kingdom is everlasting, their grief will be everlasting also. The harmless and useful servants of Christ, like the apostles, have often been troubled for their work of faith and labor of love, when wicked men have escaped. And to this day there are instances in which reading the Scriptures, social prayer, and religious conversation meet with frowns and checks. But if we obey the precepts of Christ, he will support us.

Peter being filled with the Holy Spirit wanted everyone to understand that the miracle had been done by the name or power of Jesus of Nazareth, the Messiah, whom they had crucified; and this confirmed their testimony to his resurrection from the dead, which proved him to be the Messiah. These rulers must either be saved by that Jesus whom they had crucified, or they must perish forever. The name of Jesus is given to people of every age and nation as that by which alone believers are saved from the wrath to come. But when covetousness, pride, or any corrupt passion rules within, people shut their eyes and close their hearts in hostility against the light; considering all as ignorant and unlearned who desire to know nothing compared with Christ crucified. And the followers of Christ should act so that everyone who speaks with them may recognize that they have been with Jesus. That makes them holy, heavenly, spiritual, and cheerful, and raises them above this world.

All the concern of the rulers is that the doctrine of Christ does not spread among the people, yet they cannot say it is false or dangerous, or has any ill tendency; and they are ashamed to admit the true reason: that it testifies against their hypocrisy, wickedness, and tyranny. Those who know how to put a just value upon Christ's promises, know how to put just contempt upon the world’s threatenings. The apostles look with concern on perishing souls, and know they cannot escape eternal ruin but by Jesus Christ; therefore they are faithful in warning and showing the right way. None will enjoy peace of mind nor act uprightly until they have learned to guide their conduct by the fixed standard of truth, and not by the shifting opinions and fancies of men. Especially beware of a vain attempt to serve two masters, God and the world; you will find that you can serve neither fully.

Christ's followers do best in company, provided it is their own company. It encourages God’s servants, both in doing work and suffering hardship, that they serve the God who made all things and therefore has control of all events; and the Scriptures must be fulfilled. Jesus was anointed to be a Savior, therefore it was determined he should be a sacrifice to make atonement for sin. But sin is not less evil because God brings good out of it. In threatening times, our care should not be so much that troubles may be prevented as that we may continue cheerfully and courageously in our work and duty. They do not pray, “Lord, let us go away from our work now that it has become dangerous,” but, “Lord, give us your grace to continue steadfastly in our work and not to fear the face of man.” Those who desire divine aid and encouragement may depend upon having them, and they ought to go forth and continue in the strength of the Lord God. God gave a sign of acceptance of their prayers. The place was shaken so that their faith might be established and unshaken. God gave them greater degrees of his Spirit; and they were all filled with the Holy Ghost more than ever; by which they were not only encouraged but enabled to speak the word of God with boldness. When they find the Lord God helps them by his Spirit, they know they will not be ashamed.

The disciples loved one another. This was the blessed result of Christ’s dying instruction to his disciples, and his dying prayer for them. Thus it was then, and it will be so again when the Spirit shall be poured out from above. The doctrine preached was the resurrection of Christ; a fact that, when properly explained, summarized all the duties, privileges, and comforts of Christians. There were evident results of Christ’s grace in all they said and did. They were dead to this world. This was great evidence of God's grace in them. They did not take what belonged to others, but they were indifferent to possessions. They did not consider anything as their own; because they had, in affection, forsaken everything for Christ, and expected to lose everything for following him. It is no surprise that they were of one heart and soul when they held so little regard for the wealth of this world. In effect, they shared all things common; for there was not any among them who lacked, and care was taken for their needs. The money was laid at the apostles' feet. Great care ought to be taken in distributing public charity, that it be given to those who have need, those who are not able to provide for themselves; those who are reduced to poverty through well-doing, and for the testimony of a good conscience, ought to be provided for. Here is one mentioned in particular, remarkable for his generous charity: Barnabas. As one designed to preach the gospel, he separated himself from the affairs of this life. When such dispositions prevail and are exercised according to the circumstances of the times, the testimony will have great power upon others.
